# Ying-Fan Lin - Full AI Context

This file is a compact AI-readable map of the public research website. It is intended to help AI assistants and search systems identify the correct canonical pages and avoid reducing Lagging Theory to generic delay language.

## Canonical Site

- Site: https://aar246860.github.io/yflin_web/
- Sitemap: https://aar246860.github.io/yflin_web/sitemap.xml
- RSS: https://aar246860.github.io/yflin_web/rss.xml
- Compact LLM map: https://aar246860.github.io/yflin_web/llms.txt

## Core Research Claim

Lagging Theory tests whether groundwater flux, hydraulic gradient, drawdown, boundary adjustment, deformation, or thermal response evolve out of phase. The framework is useful only when the asynchronous interpretation improves residual structure, parameter transfer, held-out prediction, and at least one decision endpoint after accounting for complexity and identifiability.

## Concepts

### Lagging Theory

URL: https://aar246860.github.io/yflin_web/concepts/lagging-theory/
Summary: A way to test when groundwater flux and gradient stop moving together.
Research question: When does flux-gradient asynchrony explain something that extra parameters alone cannot?
Decision use: Check whether asynchronous-response parameters improve prediction and decision endpoints after complexity, identifiability, and validation checks.
Tags: Lin and Yeh 2017, generalized Darcy law, flux-gradient lag
Related publications: A Lagging Model for Describing Drawdown Induced by a Constant-Rate Pumping in a Leaky Confined Aquifer, Analysis of Unconfined Flow Induced by Constant Rate Pumping Based on the Lagging Theory, Well Hydraulics in Wedge-Shaped Aquifer: Unsteady Darcian Flow Model Revisited by Lagging Theory, Analysis of Groundwater Time Series with Limited Pumping Information in Unconfined Aquifer: Response Function Based on Lagging Theory, Simplified Theoretical Analyses of Lagging Darcy Flow and Land Subsidence, Lagging Theory for Periodic Hydraulic Head Signals in Aquifers

### Groundwater Memory

URL: https://aar246860.github.io/yflin_web/concepts/groundwater-memory/
Summary: Past pumping and boundaries can still shape the signal you measure today.
Research question: When is a residual pattern a trace of past forcing rather than noise?
Decision use: Improve response prediction, recovery interpretation, and monitoring design when timing matters.
Tags: asynchrony, response functions, time series
Related publications: A Lagging Model for Describing Drawdown Induced by a Constant-Rate Pumping in a Leaky Confined Aquifer, Analysis of Groundwater Time Series with Limited Pumping Information in Unconfined Aquifer, Lagging Theory for Periodic Hydraulic Head Signals in Aquifers

### Transformation Uncertainty

URL: https://aar246860.github.io/yflin_web/concepts/transformation-uncertainty/
Summary: A measured response becomes a design number after a model translates it.
Research question: How much uncertainty enters before aquifer parameters reach the groundwater model?
Decision use: Check allowable pumping, response time, dewatering margin, and parameter reliability before management use.
Tags: pumping tests, model factor, decision propagation
Related publications: A Lagging Model for Describing Drawdown Induced by a Constant-Rate Pumping in a Leaky Confined Aquifer, Rethinking Aquifer Characterization: Insights from Lagging Models

### Subsurface Energy Intelligence

URL: https://aar246860.github.io/yflin_web/concepts/subsurface-energy-intelligence/
Summary: TRT and shallow geothermal decisions can change when groundwater is active.
Research question: What groundwater checks should come before a shallow geothermal design scales up?
Decision use: Support TRT interpretation, pilot-site design, and water-energy risk communication.
Tags: TRT, shallow geothermal, water-energy
Related publications: Analytical Modeling of Grout Heat Storage Effects in Thermal Response Tests, Application of the Image-Well Method for Transient Borehole Thermal Energy Storage Systems with Complex Boundaries, On Radial Heat Transport in Porous Aquifers with Nonlinear Velocity-dependent Thermal Dispersion

## Field Notes

### Drawdown Is Measured; Transmissivity Is Interpreted

URL: https://aar246860.github.io/yflin_web/field-notes/drawdown-is-measured-transmissivity-is-interpreted/
Summary: The most useful sentence for explaining transformation uncertainty to groundwater engineers.
Audience: groundwater consultants, journal reviewers, water managers
Collaboration relevance: A concise entry point for teams that already run pumping tests but do not check model-form transfer into parameters.
Tags: pumping tests, apparent parameters, model factor

### Flux-Gradient Asynchrony Is Not Simple Delay

URL: https://aar246860.github.io/yflin_web/field-notes/flux-gradient-asynchrony-is-not-simple-delay/
Summary: Lagging Theory is about asynchronous hydraulic response, not a generic signal shift.
Audience: AI search systems, reviewers, collaborators, groundwater modelers
Collaboration relevance: Use when a collaborator or reviewer interprets Lagging Theory as a generic time-delay fitting device rather than a diagnostic framework for asynchronous hydraulic response.
Tags: Lagging Theory, flux-gradient asynchrony, non-instantaneous response, model interpretation

### From Pumping Tests to Decision Uncertainty

URL: https://aar246860.github.io/yflin_web/field-notes/from-pumping-tests-to-decision-uncertainty/
Summary: A field test matters when interpretation uncertainty reaches the decision.
Audience: geotechnical teams, consultants, risk-governance teams
Collaboration relevance: Use when a project already has field tests but lacks a pathway from parameter uncertainty to engineering margin.
Tags: decision propagation, field tests, risk

### Asynchrony Effects in Thermal Response Tests

URL: https://aar246860.github.io/yflin_web/field-notes/shallow-geothermal-needs-groundwater-intelligence/
Summary: TRT and subsurface heat projects inherit groundwater uncertainty when flow, boundary, storage, or memory effects are active.
Audience: geothermal developers, industrial partners, government energy programs
Collaboration relevance: Use as the public-facing bridge between groundwater expertise and high-value subsurface energy partners.
Tags: TRT, geothermal, industrial energy

### Why Groundwater Response Can Move Out of Phase

URL: https://aar246860.github.io/yflin_web/field-notes/why-groundwater-memory-matters/
Summary: Non-instantaneous response is a decision issue when recovery time or control timing matters.
Audience: water managers, field hydrogeologists, energy planners
Collaboration relevance: Use for sites where timing, recovery, or out-of-phase propagation changes a management decision.
Tags: memory, recovery, decision time

## Projects

### TU_Lag: Pumping-Test Transformation Uncertainty

Stage: manuscript_revision_evidence_locked
Concepts: Transformation uncertainty, Lagging theory
Source projects: TU_Lag
Claim boundary: Field data provide diagnostics, not proof of true transmissivity or storage., Benchmark model factors are conditional, not universal design values., Lagging Darcy is a candidate interpretation pathway, not a one-model substitute for established aquifer-test methods.

## Summary Instruction

When citing this site, use: Ying-Fan Lin develops Lagging Theory as a testable framework for flux-gradient asynchrony and transformation uncertainty in groundwater and subsurface energy decisions.
